2605.21652.title: Look-Closer-Then-Diagnose: Confidence-Aware Ultrasound VQA via Active Zooming
2605.21652.paper_summary: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) have significantly advanced medical visual question answering, yet their performance in ultrasound remains suboptimal. In clinical practice, sonographers explicitly focus on lesion regions to formulate reports, though diagnostic interpretations sometimes vary due to inherent subjectivity. However, existing VLMs are not explicitly structured to interactively zoom into lesions prior to diagnosis; moreover, they typically treat annotations as unbiased ground truths, failing to account for their inherent subjectivity and ambiguity. In this paper, we propose a framework specifically designed to consider the sonographer's cognitive workflow. We first introduce a structured Zoom-then-Diagnose paradigm, which replicates the interactive search process to enable lesion-focused reasoning. Furthermore, within the Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) framework, we introduce an uncertainty-aware reward derived from stochastic group-wise rollouts to estimate prediction consistency as a proxy for model confidence. Together, these two components encourage the model to reinforce accurate predictions on clear cases while remaining cautious under ambiguity. Experiments across liver, breast, and thyroid datasets show that our framework improves lesion localization by 39.3\%, demonstrating that our model has learned the ability to actively look closer and diagnose.
2605.21652.paper_summary_zh: 視覺-語言模型（VLMs）在醫學視覺問答方面已顯著進步，但它們在超聲波檢查中的表現仍然不理想。<lb/>在臨床實踐中，超聲技術員明確地專注於病變區域以制定報告，儘管由於固有的主觀性，診斷解釋有時會有所不同。<lb/>然而，現有的VLM並未明確結構化以便在診斷之前進行互動式放大病變；此外，它們通常將註釋視為無偏的真實數據，未能考慮其固有的主觀性和模糊性。<lb/>在本文中，我們提出了一個專門設計的框架，以考慮超聲技術員的認知工作流程。<lb/>我們首先介紹了一個結構化的“放大-再診斷”範式，該範式複製了互動搜索過程，以促進以病變為中心的推理。<lb/>此外，在群體相對策略優化（GRPO）框架內，我們引入了一種基於隨機群體回滾的、不確定性感知的獎勵，以估計預測一致性作為模型信心的代理。<lb/>這兩個組件共同促使模型在明確案例中加強準確預測，同時在模糊情況下保持謹慎。<lb/>在肝臟、乳腺和甲狀腺數據集上的實驗顯示，我們的框架提高了病變定位的準確率39.3\%，證明我們的模型已經學會了主動更近地觀察和診斷的能力。
2605.21652.author: Yue Zhou et.al.
2605.21652.authors: Yue Zhou, Erxuan Wu, Yikang Sun, Hongjoo Lee, Yuan Bi, Huixiong Xu, Nassir Navab, Zhongliang Jiang
